content:
type: DEFER
files: Tests/test_image_quantize.py
spec: Owner: agent1. Please add these test(s) for the new error_threshold param on Image.quantize() in Tests/test_image_quantize.py: (1) test_error_threshold_reduces_colors: quantize a photographic image (e.g. hopper()) with colors=256, error_threshold=<a value that a simple/low-color test image undercuts easily, or use a low-detail image> and assert len(converted.getcolors()) <= 256 and that the returned image's quantize error (mean squared error between original and converted.convert('RGB')) is <= error_threshold (roughly, allow assert_image_similar style tolerance). (2) test_error_threshold_uses_max_colors_if_unmet: quantize a highly detailed image with colors=N and a very small/strict error_threshold that cannot be met within N colors, and assert the result still has <= N colors (falls back to max colors without raising). (3) test_error_threshold_default_none: confirm quantize() with error_threshold=None (default) behaves exactly as before (no behavior change) — e.g. reuse existing assertions from test_octree_quantize but passing error_threshold=None explicitly. Signature is Image.quantize(colors=256, method=None, kmeans=0, palette=None, dither=1, error_threshold=None) — error_threshold is a float or None representing max allowed MSE; when set, quantization stops adding colors once achieved MSE <= error_threshold (using fewer colors than requested if possible), or falls back to  if the threshold can't be met.
note: error_threshold quantize feature

content:
type: SPEC
files: Tests/test_image_quantize.py
spec: Owner: agent1. Please add these tests for the new error_threshold param on Image.quantize() in Tests/test_image_quantize.py: (1) test_error_threshold_reduces_colors: quantize a low-detail/solid-color-ish image with colors=256 and a lenient error_threshold, assert len(converted.getcolors()) <= 256, and assert the quantized image stays visually close to the original (e.g. via assert_image_similar with a reasonable epsilon). (2) test_error_threshold_uses_max_colors_if_unmet: quantize a highly detailed image (e.g. hopper()) with colors=N and a very strict/tiny error_threshold that cannot be met within N colors; assert it does not raise and the result has <= N colors (falls back to max colors). (3) test_error_threshold_default_none: confirm quantize() with error_threshold=None (default, unspecified) behaves exactly as existing tests expect (no behavior change) -- e.g. mirror test_octree_quantize but explicitly pass error_threshold=None. Signature: Image.quantize(colors=256, method=None, kmeans=0, palette=None, dither=1, error_threshold=None). error_threshold is a float or None representing the max allowed mean squared error between original and quantized-then-converted-back image; when set, quantization stops adding colors once achieved MSE is at or under error_threshold (possibly using fewer colors than requested), or falls back to using the full colors limit if the threshold cannot be met.
note: error_threshold quantize feature, no backticks this time

(2) test_error_threshold_uses_max_colors_if_unmet: quantize a highly detailed image (e.g. hopper()) with colors=N and a very strict/tiny error_threshold that cannot be met within N colors; assert it does not raise and the result has <= N colors (falls back to max colors). (3) test_error_threshold_default_none: confirm quantize() with error_threshold=None (default, unspecified) behaves exactly as existing tests expect (no behavior change) -- e.g. mirror test_octree_quantize but explicitly pass error_threshold=None. Signature: Image.quantize(colors=256, method=None, kmeans=0, palette=None, dither=1, error_threshold=None). error_threshold is a float or None representing the max allowed mean squared error between original and quantized-then-converted-back image; when set, quantization stops adding colors once achieved MSE is at or under error_threshold (possibly using fewer colors than requested), or falls back to using the full colors limit if the threshold cannot be met.
